Hey, welcome back and welcome to module number three, state management, which is about setting yourself up to be the absolute best you can possibly be at those key moments in life when it matters most. Now in sales, we face those so-called key moments every single day and having that ability to show up at the absolute top of your sales game on a consistent basis is one of the key distinctions that separates top producers from the rest of the pack. Now I've got to be honest with you, this is my seventh shot, actually my eighth now of taping this introduction because it's so crucial to your success, the ability to manage your state and every time I look at this intro, it doesn't capture how important this is and I want to make sure that you really get it. So I'm going to try a different tack here. But eight, go back to module one for a second. Let's talk about module one, cracking the code. Remember that certainty scale. Let's put up a graphic here and remember when your prospects enter the encounter, they show up at a relatively low level of certainty. They're not under one or two, but they're probably a four, five or six based on their preconceived notions. And the question is, where are you? Well, you're in a state of absolute certainty. What's happening is as you take someone down the straight line from the open to the close, everything you say, everything you do is designed to essentially transfer that certainty that you have to the prospect, raising them up on the scale. So boom, they get to that all important 10, 10, 10 and they buy. Makes sense, right? Well, here's a question. Where does that certainty truly come from? Does it come from the straight line system? Really think about that for a second. Does it come from the straight line? Actually, it doesn't. In other words, the straight line system is this awesome technology, love that word, right? Technology that allows for the seamless, elegant transfer of certainty from you, the salesperson, to your prospect. Elegant meaning without breaking the rules of influence. You don't break for poor, piss them off, bore them to death. You get it all you can, you must have to be able to actually take someone down the line. So it allows you to transfer this absolute certainty to the prospect, raising them up on the scale, so they buy. But it can't come from the straight line. That's not what it does. It has to come from you. The certainty comes from you, the salesperson. See, here's the deal. Bottom line, at the highest level, sales is the transference of emotion. The primary emotion that you're transferring is certainty. Certainty about your product, certainty about you, about the company, the three tens, right? What's happening is, again, your prospect enters at a lower level of certainty. You are at this ultra-high level of certainty, creates that gradient of certainty, so to speak, and that allows for this, raising them up and boom, when they get high enough and cross over their action threshold, they buy. But if you don't show up in a state of certainty, guess what? You have nothing to transfer. You get it? See, you must show up being certain, and that's what state management is all about. The best metaphor is the way a home heating system works. You have the furnace or boiler in the basement, right? Then you have all the ductwork, the HVAC system, right, that allows the heat from the furnace or boiler to move around the house, allegedly without dissipating heat, right? So you can heat up the various rooms. Makes sense. Perfect, right? Well, here's the deal. You can have this awesome ductwork, but if the furnace is broken, can you heat up a cold house? No. You're screwed. You're done. In other words, if you show up in a state of uncertainty, you have nothing to transfer, so the straight line system can't really operate. Bottom line. So this is exactly what I was referring to at the end of module two, when I said the straight line breaks down in the absence of one crucial thing, the ability to manage your state, to essentially show up in a state of absolute certainty to the encounter, so you think they use the straight line system to transfer that certainty to the prospect. Now remember, back in the day, we didn't call that state management. We called it getting negative. You didn't want to get negative, right? And in the bottom of stratton oakmont, boy, you were not allowed to get negative. I would intervene, make them stand up, walk around, change their physiology, their body language, right? So that's the hands together. You don't say, yes, I was on them because I knew intuitively, didn't call state management, I knew intuitively that if they were negative, they would not be able to close. In essence, state management is about having the ability to manage your physiological, emotional state in the moment. And that key phrase here, in the moment, it's post some extended period of time, like hours or weeks, which is basically an exercise in futility. So it's about having the ability to manage your emotional state in the moment, no matter what's going on around you, so you bring your very best self to the table and hence get the job done at the highest level, which means you close the deal. Make sense? Okay, great. Now here's the deal. While back at stratton, I didn't have a name for what I was doing. I just knew intuitively that I had to get these guys in a positive state and I had these strategies look kind of loose and fast and just stand up, do this, do that, right? Well now all of these years, ladies, I've turned it into a foolproof strategy for teaching any human being how to trigger a peak state of absolute certainty at will in those key moments. Now, I call this system olfactory anchoring, olfactory, like your nose, olfactory anchoring. I'm going to teach it to you step by step throughout the rest of this module and it's literally going to change your life, not just in sales, but also just your personal life as well because being able to show up feeling confident, clear, with patience for your children, that's truly an amazing gift to give to everyone around you, all right? So with that, I have a quiz for you even though this was the introduction because there was a lot of key content there. So please, it'll be a short quiz. There'll be one exercise back there just to get you going and then go right on to lesson number one of state of magic. This is a game changing module. I'll see you there.
